parsed_review.summary: CP00-953 (RP30.P06.M07.S02-S11, risk A, 10 units, "SensitiveGuard HRX slice") is a descriptor-only pack. Its closeout claim is hrx_sensitive_guard_m07_tail_descriptor_verified with production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only=true — it makes NO runtime or enterprise-trust claim. The claim is supported across the full chain: closeout-pack-plan.json, HRX_CP953_PACK_BINDING, HRX_CP953_REQUIREMENTS, the generated case-set/descriptor, validators.js (coverage + descriptor + contract validators), contracts/hrx-people-contract.json (current_pack/latest_pack=CP00-953, latest_projection=HrxCp953SensitiveGuardTailDescriptor, validation.valid=true, no_write_attestation all closed), and packages/hrx/test/model.test.js (genuine on-disk assertions, errors==[]). Boundary closure is enforced in createHrxDomainRows (all sensitive_guard runtime/write/real-data/bypass/policy/decision fields false; review guards h30_review_only/c30_read_only true) and the descriptor baseline_decisions/blocked_claims. Continuity is intact (CP952 M07.S01 -> CP953 S02-S11 -> CP954 S12; weighted ledger defines M07 to subphase 13). Authority boundaries hold: Claude is read-only and not final approver (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claude_is_final_approval=false); Hermes is deterministic review-packet/receipt only (emits_runtime_receipt=false, deterministic_only=true), not embedded product runtime. Review-hardening evidence is valid: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1455-byte valid-JSON stdout), prompt 2369B<25600B, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, no forbidden tools. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Provenance note: this is a read-only review; recorded command evidence (node --check/--test, npm run rp30:hrx:validate, git diff --check, all exit 0) was reported by the build agent and not independently re-executed here. Pending adjudication/construction-inspection/manifest states are the expected pre-review posture and are downstream of this review, not defects. One P3 informational finding flags that the full expected validation suite is not represented in command-evidence and must be run by the operator before finalizing closeout. This review is one input and does not constitute final closeout authority.

findings.title: Command-evidence records only HRX-scoped subset; full validation suite + gate finalization still pending operator action
findings.details: command-evidence.json records node --check (registry/validators/validate script), node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js, npm run rp30:hrx:validate, and git diff --check, all with exit_code 0 as recorded by the build agent (not re-run by this read-only review). However hermes_gate.status=pending with empty command_ids, gate_outcome=pending_pack_evidence_completion, and validation_summary.p0_p1_p2_clear=false. The broader expected pre-closeout suite (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not represented in this artifact. Correspondingly manifest.status=in_progress, construction-inspection verdict=PENDING (hrx_sensitive_guard_m07_tail_descriptor_verified=false), and adjudication.md=PENDING. These are the expected pre-review/pre-finalization states and do not indicate any defect in the descriptor-only implementation, which is correct and complete.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready_flag and finalizing adjudication/construction-inspection/manifest, the operator should execute the full expected validation suite and the Hermes deterministic gate, recording outcomes. Treat this read-only review as one input; final closeout/enterprise-trust approval remains a human authority outside this receipt.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational_no_block — expected pre-closeout workflow; does not affect descriptor-only correctness or boundary closure
